# BRIEF

## User-Facing Goal

Help people discover HostingQr through Google and understand where site visitors came from without sacrificing consent or exposing private pages to search engines.

## Success Criteria

- Search engines can discover the marketing pages through metadata, crawlable links, robots rules, and a sitemap.
- Homepage content directly explains the service and answers common questions.
- Users can accept or reject optional analytics.
- Analytics remains off until accepted and can be disabled by omitting configuration.

## UX Notes

- Reuse the existing homepage FAQ rather than add a duplicate section.
- Keep the consent notice small, plain-language, and accessible.
- Provide a persistent way to revisit the analytics choice.

## Risks Or Tradeoffs

- Google does not guarantee indexing, rankings, or AI citations.
- Rejecting analytics reduces measured traffic, by design.
- AI feature traffic is included in aggregate Google Search reporting rather than exposed as a reliable separate channel.
